steps.title: Identify the structures on each side of the blank
steps.content: When we complete the first part with scapula, we get: “The human shoulder contains the infraspinatus, a skeletal muscle that is attached to the scapula” — an independent clause. The second part, “this muscle plays a role in rotating the upper arm,” is also an independent clause. Two independent clauses need proper punctuation to join them.

steps.title: Choose the punctuation that joins two independent clauses
steps.content: A semicolon correctly joins two independent clauses. A comma alone creates a comma splice, no punctuation creates a run-on, and that is grammatically invalid. That matches choice B — B is correct.
